sanskrit:
एवमेतद्यथात्थ त्वमात्मानं परमेश्वर।
द्रष्टुं इच्छामि ते रूपमैश्वरं पुरुषोत्तम॥
transliteration:
evam etad yathāttha tvam ātmānaṃ parameśvara |
draṣṭuṃ icchāmi te rūpam aiśvaraṃ puruṣottama ||
literal_meaning: evam—thus; etat—this; yathāttha—as You have stated; tvam—You; ātmānam—Yourself; parameśvara—O Supreme Lord; draṣṭum—to see; icchāmi—I desire; te—Your; rūpam—form; aiśvaram—divine; puruṣottama—O best of beings.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna, already convinced of Krishna’s divinity, now requests to witness the cosmic form—not for personal validation but to obtain an objective vision that can later serve as a public benchmark against imposters claiming divine status.
psychological_state: A mature devotee shifts from needing proof for himself to needing demonstrable evidence for the community—an altruistic epistemic stance that balances faith with forensic responsibility.
trigger_scenario: A whistle-blower asks a respected mentor to document their methods on video—not because the whistle-blower doubts, but so future stakeholders can distinguish genuine expertise from charismatic fraud.
anchor_text: Show me Your true divine form, Lord, so all may know You.
